title: Oxypteryx curtissii
taxonomy: Oxypteryx curtissii is classified as a species within the genus Oxypteryx, which is a group of long-horned beetles belonging to the family Anisopteridae. The family Anisopteridae is a subfamily within the order Odonata, which includes dragonflies and damselflies. However, some sources may place Anisopteridae in the order Megaloptera, which includes caddisflies and allied insects. The exact classification of Oxypteryx curtissii may vary depending on the source and the current understanding of the insect's relationships. Further research is needed to confirm the classification of this species.

The plant Osha (Ligusticum porteri) is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of North America. Osha root has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing relief for colds, flu, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Osha root include sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Osha root is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried root for use in herbal supplements and traditional medicine.
